PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: three-line-note
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to write exactly three short diary sentences: fact, problem, next action. No flourish. For example: "Wall patched. Rain still gets through. I will fix the gap tomorrow."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: hard-stop-bursts
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to write abrupt bursts with hard stops and one stressed word in caps when pressure peaks. For example: "Done. Barely. The door HELD."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: image-first
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to open with one quiet image, then explain why it mattered. The image carries the feeling. For example: "Ash drifted through the lamplight. I kept sweeping after the fire was out."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: bad-then-good
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to use a clear turn: first the bad fact, then the small good thing that survived it. For example: "The roof leaked again, but the stove stayed warm."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: understated-ledger
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to write a dry tally with commas or semicolons, then end with a flat understatement. For example: "One bruise, one torn sleeve, no deaths; call that progress."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: if-unless-loop
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to write one winding sentence built around if, unless, maybe, or probably. The sentence should circle one real worry. For example: "Maybe the patch holds if the rain stops before morning."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: others-first
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to start with another person's condition before mentioning the event. Their own feelings stay indirect. For example: "Mara's hands were shaking, so I made the fire last."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: damage-report
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to write compact report clauses in this order: place, damage, survivors. For example: "South wall: cracked. Right arm: bruised. Everyone returned."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: odd-object-turn
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to begin with an odd object or sound, then pivot with "then" or "anyway" into the event. For example: "The kettle clicked twice; then the roof gave up."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: duty-sentence
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to write one formal sentence around duty, obligation, or what was owed. Keep it restrained. For example: "It was unpleasant work, but it was mine to finish."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: sour-afterthought
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to write the event plainly, then add a short sour afterthought. For example: "Help came late. I noticed."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: room-read
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to write who noticed, who looked away, and how the room shifted. No invented dialogue. For example: "Mara saw it first, and the room went careful."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: plus-minus
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to write in gain/loss terms, sometimes with plus and minus words but not literal tables. For example: "Gained a closed door. Lost one good sleeve. Fair trade."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: weather-shadow
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to open with weather, light, or shadow in a clipped sentence, then state the event. For example: "Cold morning. Dark hall. The south door finally gave."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: single-sign
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to mark one real detail as a sign, then stop before predicting anything. For example: "The birds left early. I wrote that down."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: body-measure
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to record body signals as measurements before naming feeling. For example: "Hands steady. Breath short. Fear present but manageable."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: one-flourish
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to add one dramatic phrase to an otherwise clear account. Only one flourish. For example: "A miserable little victory, but a victory all the same."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: credit-at-end
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to state the fact, then claim a little credit in the final words without changing what happened. For example: "The door held. I helped, naturally."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: old-days-contrast
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to compare the event to the old days in one sentence, then return to today. For example: "Old camps patched leaks faster; this place held anyway."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: counted-signs
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to count a small sign or habit before the event, often using a number. For example: "Three knocks before breakfast. Then the wall split."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: perhaps-question
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to turn the event into one quiet question with perhaps or one wonders. For example: "Perhaps repairs are how a place decides to stay."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: three-fragments
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to write three fragments built from concrete nouns, no explanation. For example: "Cold rail. Wet rope. Slow hands."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: repeat-pattern
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to repeat one sound, number, or word as a pattern while keeping the event visible. For example: "Three knocks. Three breaths. Door fixed."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: surreal-anchor
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to pair one strange image with one plain event fact. The fact must stay readable. For example: "The hour grew teeth; I fixed the latch."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: plain-last-image
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to write plain sentences and end on one ordinary image. For example: "We got through it. The fire was small but enough."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: soft-rhyme
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to use one small internal rhyme or beat, but no performance. For example: "Long shift, tight grip. Kept my head."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: silence-line
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to give silence its own short sentence. Keep the rest sparse. For example: "No one answered. The room did."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: hands-watching
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to describe their own body doing the event as if watched from outside. For example: "My hands finished it before I caught up."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: broken-repeat
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to write broken fragments with one repeated word. The event must remain clear. For example: "Still here. Wall held. Hold. Hold."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: already-not-yet
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: This pawn tends to use already, again, or not yet to make time feel slightly folded while the event stays clear. For example: "It felt already done before I heard the crack."
